questions.q: I can't leave the theatre — something won't let me go south.
hints: You're not leaving without your pager. It's still where you left it — down in the basement.
Answer: From the lobby go NW to the Manager's Office, DOWN through the open trapdoor, TAKE PAGER, then UP, SE, and S to leave.

questions.q: The Cloakroom looks like a dead end. Is there anything to it?
hints: Take a closer look at the hooks.
One of them is clean. Has it been used recently?
Answer: EXAMINE HOOKS (one is cleaner than the rest), then PULL CLEAN HOOK — a secret passage opens, leading down below the theatre.

questions.q: There's a closed metal safe in the Manager's Office. How do I open it?
hints: Examine it — it has a combination dial. Nobody is going to tell you the combination.
Old movies have the answer: safecrackers listen to the tumblers. You need the right instrument for that.
A stethoscope. Have you explored the upper hallways yet? There's a Sick Bay up there with a cabinet worth opening.
Answer: WEAR STETHOSCOPE, LISTEN SAFE, then TURN DIAL LEFT, TURN DIAL RIGHT, TURN DIAL LEFT, TURN DIAL RIGHT. The safe opens to reveal a red eye-like pearl. GET PEARL.

questions.q: My car's been stolen and a thug with a knife is blocking the street!
hints: You can't win this fight, and you can't run past him. The street is no longer an option.
Let him come. He isn't trying to kill you — he's herding you.
Answer: EXAMINE THUG, then WAIT three times. He drives you back into the theatre. Go N.

questions.q: There's a piano in the Music Room. Can I do anything with it?
hints: Examine it closely — note what it's standing on.
It's on wheels, and it pushes easily.
Where to push it? Think of the piano as furniture — something sturdy to stand on, positioned under something out of reach.
Answer: PUSH PIANO EAST, then PUSH PIANO SOUTH into the Prop Room. STAND ON PIANO, OPEN TRAPDOOR, and go UP into the attic.

questions.q: Is there anything I can do with the chandelier over the auditorium?
hints: You don't reach it — you bring it to you. Machinery for stage rigging is usually kept above.
The machinery is in the attic.
Answer: In the attic, TURN HANDLE. The chandelier is lowered to the auditorium floor.

questions.q: Green gas is leaking in the Attic Observatory and it's killing me!
hints: On a quick visit you can dash in and out before the gas overwhelms you — just don't linger.
To stay longer you'll need breathing protection, the sort of thing a theatre's costume stores might hold.
Answer: There's a gas mask in the Costume Room. WEAR MASK before entering.

questions.q: What is the telescope in the Observatory for?
hints: The lens isn't wrong — it just has to carry a pattern before the telescope will show you anything.
A pattern like that is burned into glass by light shining through one, not carved by hand.
Patterned light streams through a window on the Southern Landing, behind the drawn curtains.
Open them and look down — the window casts that pattern as a spotlight on the lobby floor below.
Using the telescope means standing in this gassy room, so you'll need breathing protection first.

Answer: Take the plain lens down to the lobby and PUT LENS IN SPOTLIGHT to burn the pattern in. Then WEAR MASK (the gas mask from the Costume Room), come back here, PUT LENS IN TELESCOPE — this also stops the gas — and LOOK THROUGH TELESCOPE. You collapse, and wake holding a star crystal.

questions.q: Rolls of carpet block the passage in the Narrow Hallway.
hints: They're just heavy, not magical. Shift them aside.
With the rolls moved the passage north opens up — but don't hurry past what they were lying on top of.
Steel yourself and search what's underneath; it was left there for a reason, and it's carrying something.
Answer: MOVE CARPET ROLLS to free the passage north and uncover a body. SEARCH BODY, then READ APPOINTMENT BOOK — Rienhart left his cellular phone in the theatre; find it and you can call for help.

questions.q: There's a portrait on the staircase landing — is there anything to it?
hints: It is. Get your hands on it — literally.
Answer: FEEL PAINTING, then OPEN DOOR and go N into the Secret Gallery. Take the camera you find there (GET CAMERA).

questions.q: A voice at the theatre aisle whispered that I'm "too early" and threw me out!
hints: The ghostly usher takes your ticket but the performance hasn't started. You can't wait it out — time in this place doesn't move on its own.
If time won't pass on its own, you'll need to set it yourself — find something that keeps time.
What you need is hidden close to the lobby.

Answer: With the watch in the booth: PULL DIAL, then TURN DIAL four times until it reads 7:40. Now it's late enough. Return to the aisle with your ticket — if you don't have one, there's an old ticket in the alley courtyard's rubbish bin — and DROP TICKET when the voice asks.

questions.q: Is there anything else I can do in the Manager's Office?
hints: The desk is worth a closer look — start with the drawer.
The drawer holds a set of old architect's plans — read them.
The plans show a small ticket booth once stood directly south of the office — and the wall between is just plaster and lath, not brick.
Answer: EXAMINE SOUTH WALL, then KICK SOUTH WALL. It gives way, opening an exit south into the sealed booth. Inside: a loose page, and a skeleton with rags and a pocket watch.

questions.q: I'm on the stage/backstage. What should I be doing here?
hints: Backstage, look up — there's a rope, and a door above it.
Climb the rope to the dressing room. The locker there is worth opening — carefully.
Inside the locker: an ancient amulet, a newspaper clipping... and a coiled serpent guarding them.
Don't reach in. The locker itself is your weapon — it's heavy and the stage is below.
Answer: PUSH LOCKER EAST — it falls to the stage and kills the snake. Go D, READ NEWSPAPER, GET AMULET, WEAR AMULET.

questions.q: A shadow creature east of the orchestra pit keeps driving me back!
hints: You can't fight a shadow. But think about what shadows can't survive.
Light — sudden, bright light. Have you found anything that flashes? If not, you'll need it first.

Answer: TAKE PHOTOGRAPH (with the camera from the Secret Gallery). The flash banishes the shadow. Continue east, then NW to get under the stage, where EXAMINE ITEMS turns up a green eye-like pearl among the odds and ends — GET GREEN PEARL.

questions.q: I can see a western balcony but I can't find any way to walk to it.
hints: Not every gap is crossed on foot.
If the chandelier is still hanging high, you can't cross yet — lower it first. Once it's down: a chandelier on a long chain is a pendulum.

Answer: The chandelier has to be moving first — down in the auditorium aisle, PUSH CHANDELIER to set it swinging. Then come back up to the Eastern Balcony and SWING CHANDELIER to swing across to the Western Balcony, which leads to the back wall and the Secret Library.

questions.q: A huge slug is guarding a nest, and it won't let me near!
hints: You won't get near while the slug lives — you need a way to neutralize it without touching it.
Look around the room. What's tall, heavy, and standing right next to it?
The bookcase — look closely. A long chain holds it in place.
Answer: UNHOOK CHAIN, PUSH BOOKCASE — it falls on the slug. Then EXAMINE NEST and GET BLUE PEARL.

questions.q: A crow on the theatre roof keeps flying away from me — and now it's stolen a key!
hints: You can't catch it. Follow it instead — where did it go?
Chimneys in this theatre connect rooms vertically — the fireplace in the guest star room is a way down.
In the sealed office below, there's a coffin worth opening. The crow steals the key — and drops it further down the chimney.

Answer: GET CROW — it escapes down the chimney. Follow it: D into the Guest Star Room, then CLIMB DOWN CHIMNEY into the sealed office below. OPEN COFFIN — the crow snatches the key inside and drops it further down. EXAMINE FIREPLACE, then D to the Boiler Room, where GET KEY — that shiny key unlocks a door deep underground. While you're there, OPEN CHEST and GET BOTTLE (glue), and GET LUMP from the coal bin then CLEAN LUMP, which reveals a violet eye-like pearl.

questions.q: The mannequins in the Costume Room come alive and shuffle toward me!
hints: They're slow, at least. You don't need to fight them — stop them in their tracks. Literally.
They have to cross the floor to reach you — so it's the floor you can change, not the mannequins.
Answer: BREAK BOTTLE (the glue bottle from the Boiler Room chest). The glue splashes across the floor and the mannequins stick fast. Now GET MASK — you'll need it for the gas in the Observatory.

questions.q: There's a locked door in the underground passage below the cloakroom.
hints: It's a locked door. You'll need a key.
If you haven't come across the right key yet, keep an eye out for it.
Answer: UNLOCK DOOR WITH SHINY KEY (from the Boiler Room), OPEN DOOR, and head E.

questions.q: Something huge is growling in a pit and I can't get past it alive.
hints: You can't fight it or slip past it — it's ravenous.
That hunger is the way through — give it something to eat.
Bait it with something already dead and grimly portable.
A plain meal would only sharpen its appetite and turn it on you — the bait has to be deadly.

Answer: Get the blue liquid — it's a poison — from the medical cabinet in the Sick Bay, and bring it plus Rienhart's corpse (the body from under the carpet rolls) to the pit. POUR LIQUID ON BODY, then THROW BODY IN PIT — now you can safely go DOWN.

questions.q: There's a goblin-like creature in a cage deep in the mines. Can it help me?
hints: He's more than he appears, and he knows things nobody else can tell you — particularly about certain strange objects you may have picked up along the way.
It's the eye-like pearls he cares about, and he'll want all four — one of each colour.

If you're missing any: the red is locked in the Manager's Office safe; the green is among the odds and ends under the stage; the blue is in the slug's nest in the library; the violet is hidden inside a lump of coal in the Boiler Room.
Show him things. Note down what he says — his answers are different in every playthrough, and you will need them word for word later.

Answer: SHOW VIOLET PEARL TO GOBLIN, SHOW BLUE PEARL TO GOBLIN, SHOW RED PEARL TO GOBLIN, SHOW GREEN PEARL TO GOBLIN, and WRITE DOWN how he describes each one — he doesn't name a socket, he gives each pearl a place ('stands above the others', 'forms the foundation', and so on). Which pearl gets which is randomized per game, so his words are your only key to the statue's four sockets in the Witch's Lair.

questions.q: Rats! The sewer is swarming with rats and I can't get through.
hints: You can't kill that many rats. But rats follow food. Have you been carrying anything edible?
The popcorn from the basement barrels — all four lots. Lay a trail.
Lay the popcorn trail — the western end of the sewer, the center, the eastern end, and the Rat Nest itself. The rats follow it away from where you need to be.

Answer: After laying the trail (W end, center, E end, nest), go NW then W then N, EXAMINE LEVER, WAIT until the rats reach the western end, then PULL LEVER. The sluice gate opens and flushes the rats down the drain.

questions.q: An archway keeps pushing me back — I can't get through.
hints: You can't force it, and there's no key or switch — the archway reacts to you as you try to step through.
It responds to something protective, and only if you're wearing it — carrying it isn't enough. Have you found anything like that?
Answer: WEAR AMULET (the one from the dressing-room locker behind the stage), then go SE. Worn, it lets you pass the archway into the Witch's Lair.

questions.q: It's too dark to see in the rooms beneath the theatre — I can't do anything down here.
hints: You won't find a lamp or torch to carry — that isn't how this building is lit.
The lower floors run on the theatre's own electricity, and the supply is switched off.
The controls are back where the whole night started — down in the basement.
Answer: In the basement (NW from the lobby, then DOWN), EXAMINE PANEL and TURN ON SWITCH. From then on the under-stage rooms and the underground are lit.

questions.q: What are these strange eye-like pearls actually for?
hints: They're described as eye-like. Something in the deepest part of the theatre is missing its eyes.
The statue in the Witch's Lair — look at its forehead. Four sockets in a diamond.
Each pearl has one correct socket, randomized per game — the caged goblin's description of each pearl is your key to which.

Answer: PUT each PEARL IN the SOCKET its goblin description points to — the one he called 'stands above' goes in the upper socket, 'forms the foundation' the lower, and so on (e.g. PUT BLUE PEARL IN UPPER SOCKET). When all four are placed, the statue yields a dagger. GET DAGGER, EXAMINE DAGGER.

questions.q: What do I do with this strange dagger?
hints: Look closely at the dagger — the crystal set in it can be removed. And it isn't the only crystal you've come across.
Only one of the two crystals can destroy the evil, and you need to learn which. Remove the dagger's crystal and show both it and the star crystal to the caged goblin, Trent — he can tell them apart.
The coming ceremony is a trap: Elizabeth intends to use the dagger. Make sure the crystal it holds when she takes it is the wrong one for her purpose.
Answer: GET CRYSTAL FROM DAGGER, then PUT STAR CRYSTAL IN DAGGER, ASK TRENT ABOUT ELIZABETH, then go U, NE, NE, NE and answer YES. Elizabeth takes the dagger and vanishes, releasing you.

questions.q: I'm outside a burning mansion with a man in pain. How does this end?
hints: Help the man first. You've been carrying something medicinal since the Sick Bay.
Give him the tablets. He is Trent — and he tells you what must be destroyed, and that it is below, in female form.
Take back your tools before descending — the dagger and the star crystal.

Answer: Go D to the wine cellar and THROW STAR AT YOUNG WOMAN. The crystal shatters, the evil is consumed, and you wake in the theatre lobby. Collect your pager from Rienhart in the Manager's Office (NW, D, U), then SE, S, and ENTER CAR. You have won — 50 out of 50.

questions.q: How do I get a perfect score of 50?
hints: Three of the fifty points come from the loose pages scattered everywhere — and you'll want them all before the ceremony.

There are pages in the basement, hallways, attic, alley bin, secret gallery, reading recess, ticket booth, backstage, under the stage, the underground passages, and the Witch's Lair. If you've missed any, go back for them now.
Answer: With every loose page collected, READ PAGES (read all of them) before the ceremony. Reading the full set is worth 3 points; the rest come from solving the game's puzzles.
